summary: Comprehensive end-to-end test of Zero+ web port (backend + frontend). All 11 backend pytest cases passed; all 25+ frontend flows verified via Playwright at the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Live crypto, stocks, fx, news, device-settings sync all functional. UI shell, omnibar, all 5 dock apps (Boba/Pomodoro/FlipClock/Stocks/News), settings drawer, social sidebar, bookmarks, todo, notepad all working. Zero console errors. One minor non-blocking observation about the min+reopen+close window lifecycle.

ui_bugs.issue: In a sequential open->min->reopen-via-dock->close test, the close button click did not remove the window from DOM (count remained 1). When tested in isolation (open->close, no min), close works correctly (count goes to 0). May indicate a stale state after the minimize/reopen cycle. Reproducible only in long-running sequence; not reproducible from a fresh page.

action_items: (Optional) Investigate FloatingWindow close behaviour after a minimize+reopen cycle to make sure close always removes the window (currently passes isolated but flagged a stale state in long sequence).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: news topic 'world' includes feeds.reuters.com which has been deprecated by Reuters and frequently 404s — recommend removing or replacing.
server.py: _yf_lookup uses yfinance which is slow + rate-limited; cache TTL is only 30s; consider increasing TTL or batch caching per-symbol for resilience.
server.py: in-memory _cache is not bounded — long-running container could grow. Add a max-entries cap or use cachetools.TTLCache.
server.py uses deprecated FastAPI @app.on_event('shutdown'); replace with lifespan handler in a future cleanup.

success_rate.frontend: ~100% (all targeted flows verified; one low-severity sequencing edge case)

test_credentials: N/A — app is anonymous (device-id only); /app/memory/test_credentials.md not required.
seed_data_creation: Test PUT /api/settings/{TEST_uuid} created one test device-settings document; benign.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression suite lives at /app/backend/tests/test_zeroplus_backend.py — re-run with `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_zeroplus_backend.py -v`. Public URL https://zero-hub-3.preview.emergentagent.com. Frontend uses anonymous device_id stored in localStorage; settings sync debounced (~2s). Stocks app loads data on window open (wait 5–8s after dock-item-stocks click). CoinGecko occasionally returns 502 from datacenter IPs — backend retries 3x and caches stale responses, so it remained stable through the run.
rca of the issue: N/A — no blocking bugs found. The single LOW-priority window-lifecycle observation: in a long sequence (open -> minimize -> reopen via dock -> close), the close button did not remove the window from DOM. Hypothesis: ZeroProvider's window reducer may treat dock-click-during-minimized as a 'focus/restore' rather than create, but the close action then fails to fully unmount because of a stale wid reference. Re-test in isolation succeeded, so reducer state is sensitive to the order of (minimize, dock-reopen, close). Recommend a unit-level check of the reducer's WIN_CLOSE handling and the dock click handler when a window is in `minimized=true` state.
